in: Weapons in Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis, Weapon lists

Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis weapons

Sign In to Save Save Edit
  • Edit source
  • History
  • Purge
  • Talk (9)
Barret: There's no gettin' off this train we on 'till we reach the end of the line.
This article is about equipment that's continually being updated. As such, some of the information might be inaccurate or likely to change. Please look over our policy for updating articles covering live content before editing this page.
Cid Pollendina: Oh, shut up and help me remodel the Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis weapons page!
Please expand this article into a full one. The following tasks need to be completed:
  • Keep all lists current.
  • Update for Level 130.
  • Guild exchanges
  • Make room for 3★-4★ abilities on each weapon (useful for bad pulls).

This request can be discussed on the associated discussion page. Remove this notice upon completion.

Weapons (武器, Buki?) in Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis are obtained primarily through the gacha system, where players can draw for a chance of pulling each weapon, and through the exchange systems that are updated periodically. Each character can equip a main weapon and a sub-weapon ("ability weapon") unique to that character, as well as three sub-equipment weapons chosen from any owned weapons.

This list does not include Ultimate Weapons, which, though obtained by many of the same methods, are mechanically distinct from those in the common pool.

Contents

  • 1 Abilities
    • 1.1 Command abilities
    • 1.2 Reinforcement abilities
    • 1.3 Support abilities
    • 1.4 Customization abilities
  • 2 Level caps
  • 3 Upgrading rarity and Overboost
    • 3.1 Parts conversion rates
  • 4 List of weapons
    • 4.1 Cloud's broadswords
    • 4.2 Barret's gun-arms
    • 4.3 Tifa's knuckles
    • 4.4 Aerith's staves
    • 4.5 Red XIII's collars
    • 4.6 Yuffie's shuriken
    • 4.7 Cait Sith's megaphones
    • 4.8 Vincent's guns
    • 4.9 Cid's spears
    • 4.10 Zack's broadswords
    • 4.11 Sephiroth's katana
    • 4.12 Glenn's blades
    • 4.13 Matt's bayonets
    • 4.14 Lucia's rifles
    • 4.15 Angeal's broadswords
    • 4.16 Sephiroth (Original)'s katana

Abilities[]

In addition to base stats, weapons have one command ability and two reinforcement abilities.

Command abilities[]

A command ability (C. Ability) is the unique ability assigned to that weapon. At the lowest possible rarity rank of 3★, command abilities are at best amplified versions of skills already present on materia. By advancing the rarity rank of the weapon, the player can transform and upgrade its associated command ability into newer and more powerful forms. At 5★ and above, the command ability has reached maximum evolution potential, but can be upgraded to + and ++ grades alongside the attributes of the linked weapon. Upon reaching 15★, the command ability reaches its maximum potential ability level. Further overboosting can raise raw stats to even higher levels. Both the main and sub-weapon's command abilities are slotted into the first two slots of a character's ATB command list in battle.

Reinforcement abilities[]

Reinforcement abilities (R. Abilities) provide secondary passives to a character. All equipped weapons' reinforcement abilities are equipped onto the character, but the level of each ability is heavily affected by whether it is a main or sub-weapon. Each reinforcement ability is assigned a predetermined amount of points which determine their level, and equipping a weapon as a sub-weapon or sub-equipment weapon halves the reinforcement points. All weapons begin with only their command ability unlocked, with each level uncap unlocking or enhancing the remaining abilities including support materia (below).

Support abilities[]

Support abilities (S. Abilities) can further enhance equipped abilities and indicate which sigil the weapon can deal bonus damage to. All weapons possess three support abilities that are unlocked as the weapons' level caps are raised.

Customization abilities[]

Customization is a special enhancement only available to certain weapons. You can consume items to enhance these weapons further with this feature. Available customizations and effects vary according to each weapon. Weapons that have already been customized can have additional customizations unlocked by consuming items. The weapon can then switch between unlocked customizations.

  • Switching between unlocked customizations incurs no item cost.

There are three types of Customization along with its' functions. Which is:

  • Heart Customization: Power-up C. abilities.
  • Spade Customization: Added C. abilities an effect.
  • Diamond Customization: Added R. abilities.

Level caps[]

All weapons begin with an initial level cap at level 20, raised by collecting special upgrade materials dropped on mission completion or acquired via expeditions and exchanges. Each cap break raises the weapon's maximum level by 10 in addition to unlocking one skill or support materia. However, each rarity rank has a maximum level cap as follows:

  • 3★: Lv. 50.
  • 4★: Lv. 70.
  • 5★-15★+: Lv. 140.

Upgrading rarity and Overboost[]

All weapons can be evolved in rarity with weapon parts converted from draws or acquired at an exchange as follows:

  • 3★-4★: 10 parts needed
  • 4★-15★+:200 parts needed per level evolved.

A weapon enters Overboost upon breaking 5★ by any available method. Once the weapon reaches 11★ (referred to by players as "OB6"), its assigned C. Ability gains a rank and its stats improve dramatically. At a full 15★ ("OB10"), another rank is added. From this point forward, the weapon enters Overboost+ and can be augmented up to +20, with each level raised alternating one of two stats to upgrade.

Parts conversion rates[]

In the event that the player has already acquired any given weapon by any available means, all future pickups of the same weapon will convert to parts as follows with automatic conversion enabled:

  • 3★: x1
  • 4★: x10
  • 5★: x200

List of weapons[]

Stats shown are for weapons equipped as Main at Max. Tier (Lv. 130 and ★★★★★ rarity).

Cloud's broadswords[]

Final Fantasy VII Ever Crisis
Content Artwork - Translations
Adapted Stories Final Fantasy VII - Crisis Core - The First Soldier (Episode I - Episode II) - Advent Children - Dirge of Cerberus
Locations Banora - Cawpine Cavern - Chocobo Farm - Fort Tamblin - Grastia Path - La'paina Coast - Logues Ruins - Midgar - Midgar Slums - Modeoheim - Nibelheim - Padapili Sea Cave - Rhadore Archipelago - Shinra Building - Sijad - Sunsin Cave - Wall Market - Wutai
GameplayFeatures Characters - Events - Battle Towers - Crisis Dungeons - Criterion Dungeons - Quests - Story
Inventory Collection - Gear - Home Wallpapers - Items - Materia - Memoria - Ultimate Weapons - Weapons
Mechanics Achievements - Abilities - Badges - Expeditions - Gacha - Guilds - Instant Items - Limit Break - Missions - Overspeed - Rare enemies - Roles - Sigils - Statstream - Statuses
Summons Ifrit - Shiva - Ramuh - Bahamut - Ifrit & Bahamut - Leviathan - Shiva & Ramuh - Titan - Odin - Leviathan & Bahamut
Enemies Enemies - Enemy abilities
